diagnostics.title: BaaS authority map shipped in browser artifact
diagnostics.message: A browser artifact exposes Firebase/Supabase config together with sensitive collections or authorization fields.
diagnostics.help: Client BaaS config is often public, but shipped collection names plus owner, role, tenant, or admin fields give attackers a precise authorization map. Verify rules/RLS enforce every boundary server-side.

diagnostics.message: Unused dependency: `dotenv`
diagnostics.help: An unused dependency adds install time and supply-chain surface without being used; remove it from package.json if it is genuinely unused.

diagnostics.message: Unused file is not reachable from any entry point, so it adds maintenance surface without shipping any code.
diagnostics.help: Delete the file if it is truly unreachable, or import it from an entry point.

diagnostics.message: Unused file is not reachable from any entry point, so it adds maintenance surface without shipping any code.
diagnostics.help: Delete the file if it is truly unreachable, or import it from an entry point.

diagnostics.title: Handler on non-interactive element
diagnostics.message: Keyboard & screen reader users can't trigger this `<li>` because it isn't interactive, so use a button or link or add an interactive role.
diagnostics.help: Put interactions on a button or link, or add an interactive role.

diagnostics.message: Unused file is not reachable from any entry point, so it adds maintenance surface without shipping any code.
diagnostics.help: Delete the file if it is truly unreachable, or import it from an entry point.

diagnostics.title: Permanent will-change wastes GPU
diagnostics.message: This wastes GPU memory because will-change is left on all the time, so add it right before the animation & remove it when the animation ends
diagnostics.help: Add will-change when the animation starts (`onMouseEnter`) and remove it when it ends (`onAnimationEnd`). Leaving it on all the time wastes GPU memory and can slow things down

diagnostics.title: Large component is hard to read and change
diagnostics.message: Component "SynonymManagerModal" is over 300 lines long, which is hard to read & change. Split it into a few smaller components.
diagnostics.help: Pull each section into its own component so the parent is easier to read, test, and change.

diagnostics.title: Chained array iterations
diagnostics.message: This loops over your list twice because .filter().map() makes two passes, so do it in one pass with .reduce() or a for...of loop
diagnostics.help: Combine `.map().filter()` style chains into one pass with `.reduce()` or a `for...of` loop, so you only loop over the list once

diagnostics.title: Control missing accessible label
diagnostics.message: Blind users can't tell what this control does because screen readers find no label, so add visible text, `aria-label`, or `aria-labelledby`.
diagnostics.help: Give every interactive control a label screen readers can read.

diagnostics.title: Heavy library loaded eagerly
diagnostics.message: "recharts" ships extra code to your users up front & slows page load. Load it on demand with React.lazy() or next/dynamic.
diagnostics.help: Load it only when needed: `const Component = dynamic(() => import('library'), { ssr: false })` from next/dynamic, or React.lazy().

diagnostics.title: Inline prop defeats memo()
diagnostics.message: This redraws PeriodButton on every render because the prop is functions built right here, so memo() can't skip it. Move it to a stable value with useMemo, useCallback, or module scope
diagnostics.help: Move the inline `() => ...` / `[]` / `{}` to a stable value with useMemo, useCallback, or module scope, so the memoized child stops redrawing on every parent render

diagnostics.message: Unused export: `AnimatedNumber` is exported but no module imports it, so it expands the public surface and can mislead callers about supported API.
diagnostics.help: Drop the `export` keyword (or remove the declaration) if no other module uses this symbol.

diagnostics.title: Large component is hard to read and change
diagnostics.message: Component "ReservasExpandedRow" is over 300 lines long, which is hard to read & change. Split it into a few smaller components.
diagnostics.help: Pull each section into its own component so the parent is easier to read, test, and change.

diagnostics.title: Unstable context provider value
diagnostics.message: Every reader of this context redraws on each render because you build its `value` inline.
diagnostics.help: Wrap the context value in `useMemo` or move it outside the component so consumers do not redraw every render.

diagnostics.title: Unversioned localStorage key
diagnostics.message: localStorage.setItem("ignoredDuplicatesTrigram", JSON.stringify(...)) has no version, so changing the data shape later crashes your users' saved sessions. Add one to the key (e.g. "ignoredDuplicatesTrigram:v1").
diagnostics.help: Put a version in the storage key (e.g. "myKey:v1"). If you change the data shape later, old saved data can be ignored instead of crashing the app.

diagnostics.title: Array lookup inside a loop
diagnostics.message: This scales poorly because `array.includes()` inside a loop scans the whole list every time. Use a Set for constant-time lookups.
diagnostics.help: Use a `Set` or `Map` when you check for the same items over and over. `Array.includes`/`find` scans the whole list each time

diagnostics.title: Spread copy before sort()
diagnostics.message: This wastes work because [...array].sort() copies the array just to sort it, so use array.toSorted() to sort without the extra copy (ES2023)
diagnostics.help: Use `array.toSorted()` (ES2023) instead of `[...array].sort()` so you sort without copying the array first

diagnostics.title: Role used instead of HTML tag
diagnostics.message: Screen reader users get more reliable semantics from `<button>` than `role="button"`, so use `<button>` instead.
diagnostics.help: Use the matching HTML element when one exists so browsers and assistive tech get native semantics.

diagnostics.title: Array index used as a key
diagnostics.message: Your users can see & submit the wrong data when this list reorders or filters, so use a stable id like `key={item.id}`, not the array index "i".
diagnostics.help: Use a stable id from the item, like `key={item.id}` or `key={item.slug}`. Index keys break when the list reorders or filters.

diagnostics.title: Large component is hard to read and change

diagnostics.title: Boolean prop combinations are hard to test
diagnostics.message: Component "ProductosHeaderBar" takes 4 on/off props (isSyncingPrecios, isExporting, isSyncing…), which is hard to combine & test. Split it into smaller components or named variants.
diagnostics.help: Split boolean-heavy APIs into smaller components or named variants so combinations stay testable.

diagnostics.title: Prefer explicit variant components
diagnostics.message: Component "ProductosHeaderBar" picks which component to render from 4 boolean props (isSyncingPrecios, isExporting, isSyncing…), which multiplies untestable variants. Split it into explicit variant components so each renders one clear path.
diagnostics.help: Replace boolean props that switch whole subtrees with explicit variant components, like `<ThreadComposer />` and `<EditMessageComposer />`, so each variant renders one clear path.

diagnostics.title: Heavy library loaded eagerly
diagnostics.message: "recharts" ships extra code to your users up front & slows page load. Load it on demand with React.lazy() or next/dynamic.
diagnostics.help: Load it only when needed: `const Component = dynamic(() => import('library'), { ssr: false })` from next/dynamic, or React.lazy().

diagnostics.title: Role used instead of HTML tag
diagnostics.message: Screen reader users get more reliable semantics from `<button>` than `role="button"`, so use `<button>` instead.
diagnostics.help: Use the matching HTML element when one exists so browsers and assistive tech get native semantics.

diagnostics.title: Heavy library loaded eagerly
diagnostics.message: "recharts" ships extra code to your users up front & slows page load. Load it on demand with React.lazy() or next/dynamic.
diagnostics.help: Load it only when needed: `const Component = dynamic(() => import('library'), { ssr: false })` from next/dynamic, or React.lazy().

diagnostics.title: Array index used as a key
diagnostics.message: Your users can see & submit the wrong data when this list reorders or filters, so use a stable id like `key={item.id}`, not the array index "index".
diagnostics.help: Use a stable id from the item, like `key={item.id}` or `key={item.slug}`. Index keys break when the list reorders or filters.

diagnostics.title: Supabase table created without Row Level Security
diagnostics.message: Supabase migration creates a public table but never enables Row Level Security, leaving every row exposed to the anon key.
diagnostics.help: Enable RLS in the same migration (`alter table <name> enable row level security;`) and add `auth.uid()`-scoped policies for select/insert/update/delete. A public table without RLS is fully readable and writable with the public anon key.

diagnostics.title: Permissive Supabase RLS policy
diagnostics.message: Supabase policy SQL disables RLS, permits writes broadly, or references a service-role bypass.
diagnostics.help: Keep public-read policies explicit, but gate inserts, updates, deletes, and service-role bypasses behind `auth.uid()` plus trusted tenant membership.

diagnostics.title: BaaS authority map shipped in browser artifact
diagnostics.message: A browser artifact exposes Firebase/Supabase config together with sensitive collections or authorization fields.
diagnostics.help: Client BaaS config is often public, but shipped collection names plus owner, role, tenant, or admin fields give attackers a precise authorization map. Verify rules/RLS enforce every boundary server-side.
